info.title: Oxy REST API
info.description: The Oxy REST API powers every official Oxy app (web, iOS, Android) and is open to third-party integrations via the developer apps program. It covers authentication, accounts, devices, files, billing, notifications, email, federation (ActivityPub), and developer tooling.

bearerAuth.description: User session JWT obtained from /auth/login, /auth/signup, or /auth/verify. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

serviceTokenAuth.description: Service-to-service JWT obtained from /auth/service-token by exchanging DeveloperApp credentials. For delegated calls also include `X-Oxy-User-Id: <userId>`.

apiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
apiKeyAuth.description: Long-lived developer app API key (for third-party integrations that do not require a delegated user session).

csrfToken.name: X-CSRF-Token
csrfToken.description: Required for state-changing requests against CSRF-protected routes. Obtain a token from any GET response (set via the `XSRF-TOKEN` cookie) and echo it on subsequent POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE calls. Only enforced when cookies are present.

total.description: Total number of records matching the query.

limit.description: Page size used for this response.

offset.description: Zero-based offset applied to this page.

avatar.description: Avatar asset URL or asset ID.

type.description: Account type. Local users are first-party Oxy accounts; federated users come from ActivityPub instances; agents / automated accounts are programmatic.

Device.description: A device that has at least one active session.

accessToken.description: Short-lived JWT for API calls. Typically valid 15 minutes.

refreshToken.description: Long-lived refresh token. Submit to /auth/refresh for a new access token.

get.description: Paginated list of files owned by the authenticated user, including upload status, deduplication info (`sha256`), variants (thumbnails, resized renditions), and any entity links.

post.description: First call in the two-step upload flow. Submit the file's SHA-256, size, and MIME type and receive a `fileId` plus a pre-signed S3 upload URL. If a file with the same SHA-256 already exists for the caller, the server short-circuits and returns the existing record (de-duplication).

deduplicated.description: True if the SHA-256 was already present.

post.description: Second call in the two-step upload flow. Call this after the client has finished PUTing the file to the pre-signed S3 URL returned by `/assets/init`. Commits the metadata, marks the file ready, and enqueues variant generation (thumbnails, image resizes) where applicable.

post.summary: Upload a file in a single request
post.description: Convenience endpoint that wraps the init/PUT/complete flow into a single multipart upload. The backend computes the SHA-256 itself. Use this for small files where the round-trip cost outweighs the benefit of direct-to-S3 streaming.

get.description: Return metadata for the file (size, MIME, SHA-256, variants, links, usage count, status). Only the owner can fetch metadata; signed URLs (`/assets/:id/url` or `/assets/:id/stream`) are the public access path.

delete.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/assets.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

get.summary: Get a download URL for a file
get.description: Return a CDN URL (for public files) or a pre-signed S3 URL (for private files) suitable for `` or direct download. The URL is valid for `expiresIn` seconds (default 3600).

schema.description: Seconds the signed URL stays valid.

get.description: Public endpoint that streams the raw file bytes back to the caller. Sends correct `Content-Type` and `Cache-Control` headers and falls back to a placeholder if the underlying object is missing (`?fallback=placeholder|placeholderVisible|icon`). For private files the caller must supply a bearer token.

200.description: File bytes streamed (or placeholder if fallback requested).

404.description: File not found (and no fallback requested).

post.description: Create a new local Oxy account from an email + username + password triple. On success returns the user record plus an `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, and a `sessionId` for the newly created session. Username is normalised to lowercase and must match the `^[a-zA-Z0-9]{3,30}$` shape.

name.description: Optional human-readable name for the account.

deviceName.description: Friendly device label. Shown to the user in the devices list.

deviceFingerprint.description: Stable per-device identifier used for de-duplicating sessions.

200.description: Account created and the first session issued.

429.description: Too many signups from this IP. Try again later.

post.summary: Log in with email or username and password
post.description: Exchange a password for a session. Accepts either `identifier` (email-or-username), or one of the dedicated `email` / `username` fields plus `password`. If the account has TOTP-based 2FA enabled the response will return a `loginToken` to be completed via `POST /security/2fa/verify-login` instead of the regular tokens.

identifier.description: Email or username. Preferred over the separate fields.

200.description: Login succeeded. Returns either an `AuthSuccess` payload (no 2FA) or `{ twoFactorRequired: true, loginToken }` for accounts with 2FA enabled.

429.description: Too many failed login attempts. Try again later.

post.description: Send a password recovery code to the user's email.

200.description: Recovery code sent (returns success even if account not found to prevent enumeration)

post.description: Verify the recovery code sent via email and receive a reset token.
Reset the user's password using a valid recovery token.

post.summary: Register a new account with a public key
post.description: Create a passwordless account bound to a local secp256k1 identity. The client generates a key pair (see `KeyManager` in `@oxyhq/core`), signs `register:{publicKey}:{timestamp}`, and submits the signature. Username and email are optional but recommended for discoverability.

timestamp.description: Unix ms when the signature was produced (max 5 minutes old).

200.description: Account created and the first session issued.

400.description: Invalid signature, malformed key, or duplicate username/email.

post.summary: Request a sign-in challenge for a public key
post.description: Step 1 of the passwordless public-key login. Returns a short-lived random challenge that the client must sign with the matching private key, then submit to `POST /auth/verify`.

404.description: No account registered for this public key.

post.summary: Verify a signed challenge and create a session
post.description: Step 2 of the passwordless public-key login. Submit the challenge returned by `/auth/challenge` together with its signature; on success a new session is created and the standard `AuthSuccess` payload is returned.

get.description: Check whether the current request carries valid authentication.
Check whether a username is available for registration.
Lightweight lookup that returns minimal public info for the login flow. Returns whether the user exists along with their color preset, avatar, and display name.
Check whether an email address is available for registration.

post.summary: Open a cross-app auth session (OAuth-like flow)
post.description: Begin a cross-app sign-in handshake. A third-party / first-party client generates a one-time `sessionToken` and opens this endpoint; the user is then directed to Oxy Accounts where they authorise the session via `POST /auth/session/authorize/{sessionToken}`. The client polls `GET /auth/session/status/{sessionToken}` until the session is authorised, cancelled, or expires (default 5 minutes).

sessionToken.description: Random opaque token the client generates and keeps secret.

400.description: Missing field or token already in use.

get.summary: Poll the status of a cross-app auth session
get.description: Polled by the client that opened a session via `POST /auth/session/create`. Returns the session's current `status` and, once authorised, the `sessionId` / `userId` that the client should now treat as its own session.

200.description: Current status of the auth session.

post.summary: Authorise a pending cross-app auth session
post.description: Called by the Oxy Accounts app (or any first-party UI) after the user accepts a cross-app sign-in prompt. Requires the authorising user's access token via the `Authorization: Bearer` header — the authenticated principal is the only valid source of "who is authorising". The previous `x-session-id`-based path has been removed (fixes C2).

404.description: Auth session not found or already processed.

post.summary: Issue an OAuth2 authorization code (after user consent)
post.description:
Called by the Oxy auth UI after the user clicks "Allow" on a third-party app's consent screen. Requires the authenticated user's Bearer access token. Returns a short-lived single-use code that the client app exchanges for tokens via `POST /auth/oauth/token`.
The `redirectUri` MUST exactly match one of the DeveloperApp's registered `redirectUris` — otherwise the request is rejected.

state.description: Opaque CSRF token forwarded back to the client.

codeChallenge.description: PKCE code challenge (S256). Required for public clients.

403.description: Redirect URI is not registered for this client.

post.summary: Exchange an OAuth2 authorization code for tokens
post.description:
Single-use exchange of an authorization code (from `POST /auth/oauth/authorize`) for a bearer access token, refresh token, and session ID. Either `clientSecret` (confidential clients) or `codeVerifier` (public clients with PKCE) is required.
Replaying an already-used code, sending a code past its 60-second TTL, or mismatching the `redirectUri` returns 401 with no detail about why.

401.description: Invalid, expired, replayed, or mis-bound code.

post.summary: Exchange credentials for a service token
post.description: Internal service-to-service authentication endpoint. Exchange DeveloperApp credentials (apiKey + apiSecret) for a short-lived service JWT (1 hour). Only available to apps with isInternal flag set.

403.description: App is not an internal service

post.summary: Discover which contacts are on Oxy
post.description:
Privacy-preserving contact-book matching. Clients SHA-256 hash each email and phone number locally (lowercased; phone numbers normalised to E.164 — see `utils/contactHash.ts` in `@oxyhq/core`) and upload only the resulting 64-character hex digests. The server intersects them against precomputed indexes on the `User` collection and returns matched Oxy user IDs.
Privacy invariants:
  - Raw email / phone never traverses this endpoint.
  - The response contains no PII — only Oxy user IDs and the hash the
    caller supplied (so the client can map matches back to the local
    contact that produced them).
  - The endpoint never writes to the database. Discovery is stateless.

Rate limits: 5 requests per minute per authenticated user; up to 200 hashed identifiers per channel (~400 total) per request. Service tokens are explicitly rejected with 403.

items.description: SHA-256 hex of the E.164-normalised phone number.

200.description: Discovery completed. Empty `matches` means no overlap.

400.description: Validation failed (empty payload, malformed hash, oversized batch).

403.description: Service token used — must use a user session token.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (5 requests / minute / user).

get.description: Return every device that has at least one active session for the authenticated user. Useful for "Where am I signed in?" screens.

get.summary: Get aggregate security info for the user's devices
get.description: Return high-level security signals across the user's devices (count of active sessions, unusual logins, last suspicious activity, etc.). Used to surface a security health summary on the account dashboard.

delete.description: Revoke every active session attached to the given device. The user on that device will be signed out the next time their client tries to refresh its access token.

post.description: The auth UI requests a server-issued nonce just before invoking `navigator.credentials.get({ identity: { nonce } })`. The IdP signs the nonce into the issued ID token; `/fedcm/exchange` will only accept tokens whose nonce was minted here and has not yet been used. Bound to the requesting origin so a nonce minted for one site cannot be exchanged from another.

post.summary: Exchange a FedCM ID token for an Oxy session
post.description: Implements the FedCM (Federated Credential Management) token exchange. The relying party passes the ID token it just received from the browser's FedCM API and gets back an Oxy `AuthSuccess` payload (user + sessionId + access/refresh tokens). This is the cookie-less, cross-origin SSO entry point.

idToken.description: FedCM ID token from the browser.

403.description: Origin not on the approved-clients list.

get.summary: List origins approved for FedCM token exchange
get.description: Public list of `origin` strings that may call `/fedcm/exchange`. Browsers query this list when displaying the FedCM account picker.

post.summary: Approve an origin for FedCM (internal services only)
post.description: Add a new origin to the FedCM approved-clients list. Requires a service token — third-party developers cannot self-approve.

403.description: Token is not a service token.

delete.summary: Remove an approved origin (internal services only)

403.description: Token is not a service token.

get.description: Return the full privacy settings record for the user. Some fields are only visible to the owner; non-owner callers see a redacted projection.

patch.summary: Update a user's privacy settings (owner only)
patch.description: Partial update of the user's privacy settings. Only the owner of the account may patch their settings — other callers get 403. Invalidates the in-memory user cache so subsequent reads return fresh values.

tighten.summary: Make profile private and disable email discovery

get.description: Return the users the authenticated caller has blocked.

post.description: Block `targetId` for the authenticated caller. Blocking is symmetric — neither account can see the other's posts, profile, or reach the other in DMs.

get.description: Return the users the authenticated caller has restricted.

post.description: Restrict `targetId` — they can still see public content but their replies and DMs are silently filtered out of the caller's view.

get.description:
Resolve a username to a public profile, with follower/following counts. Federated handles (`user@domain`) are resolved on the fly via WebFinger + ActivityPub; if the actor has never been seen the endpoint will upsert it as a federated user before returning.
This endpoint is unauthenticated and may be cached by edge.

schema.description: Local username (alphanumeric, 3-30 chars) or fediverse handle.

404.description: Profile not found (and federation lookup failed).

get.description: Return security-relevant events for the authenticated user — sign-in attempts, password changes, 2FA toggles, private-key exports, backup creations, suspicious activity flags. Used to power the activity history screen.

schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by the previous page.

post.description: Record that the local identity wallet exported its private key for backup. The event surfaces in the activity log and is used to remind the user to safely store the key.

deviceId.description: Device that performed the export. Optional.

post.description: Record that the local identity wallet wrote a backup of its keys.

get.summary: Get 2FA status for the current user
get.description: Return whether TOTP-based two-factor authentication is enabled, when it was set up, and how many backup codes remain.

post.description: Generate a TOTP secret and otpauth URI for the user to register in their authenticator app. The secret is provisional until confirmed via `POST /security/2fa/enable` with a valid TOTP code.

post.summary: Confirm 2FA setup and turn it on
post.description: Submit a fresh TOTP code from the user's authenticator app to confirm the provisional secret. On success returns 10 backup codes (single-use, downloadable).
Turn 2FA off. Requires the account password and, if currently enabled, a fresh TOTP code or backup code.
Used to validate a TOTP code outside the login flow — for example, to allow a sensitive action to proceed. Accepts either a fresh TOTP code or a backup code.

identifier.description: Email or username of the user being verified.

post.description: Final step of the password + 2FA login flow. Submit the `loginToken` returned by `/auth/login` along with a TOTP or backup code. On success returns the standard `AuthSuccess` payload.
Replace the existing backup codes with a fresh set of 10. Invalidates every previously issued backup code.

get.summary: Resolve a session ID to the user it belongs to
get.description: Look up the user record for a given session ID. Requires a valid access token whose user owns the referenced session — callers cannot look up sessions belonging to other users. Used by SDK clients (e.g. `@oxyhq/core`) to hydrate user state from a stored session reference. Expired, revoked, or non-owned sessions return 404.

get.summary: Issue a fresh access token from a session ID
get.description: Exchange a long-lived session ID for a short-lived access token (typically 15 minutes). Requires a valid access token whose user owns the referenced session — callers cannot mint tokens for sessions belonging to other users.

get.summary: List all sessions for the user of the given session
get.description: Return every active session the user has across all devices, with a flag marking the current session. Requires a valid access token whose user owns the referenced session.

post.description: Revoke the session identified by `sessionId`. After this call any bearer token tied to that session is rejected. Idempotent — calling twice is a no-op on the second call.

post.summary: Sign another session out via an authenticated session
post.description: Use `sessionId` (which must be a valid active session) to revoke a different session identified by `targetSessionId`. This is how the "sign out other devices" flow works.

401.description: The acting session is not valid.

post.summary: Sign out every session for this user
post.description: Revoke every session belonging to the user behind `sessionId`, including the calling session itself. Recommended after a password reset or suspected compromise.

get.description: Cheap liveness check — returns 200 with a small payload if the session is still active, 404 otherwise. Use this in lieu of the heavier `/session/user/:id` lookup when you only need a yes/no.

get.summary: Validate a session using a header-bound bearer token
get.description: Like `/session/validate/:sessionId` but cross-checks the bearer token against the session — returns 200 only if the token still belongs to the session.

401.description: Token does not match the session.

get.summary: List sessions on the same device as the given session
get.description: Return every active session that shares the device fingerprint of the supplied session. Used to power "this device has these accounts" UI in the auth picker.

post.summary: Sign out every session on this device
post.description: Revoke every session that shares the device fingerprint of the supplied session. Useful for a one-click "sign out this device" button.

put.summary: Rename the device for the given session
put.description: Update the human-readable device label shown in the user's devices list. The change applies to every session sharing the device fingerprint.

post.summary: Resolve multiple session IDs in one call
post.description: Batch counterpart of `/session/user/:sessionId`. Pass an array of session IDs and get back a map of user records — used by clients that need to render multiple connected accounts at once (e.g. the account switcher).

200.description: User records keyed by session ID. Missing sessions are omitted.

get.summary: Get the authenticated user's storage usage
get.description: Return the total bytes used and the user's storage quota for the file-storage feature. Used by the storage settings UI to render progress bars and quota warnings.

Returns the full profile for the bearer-token holder, including privacy settings, identity flags, and connected account types. This is the canonical "who am I" endpoint that every Oxy app calls on startup.

put.description: Partial profile update for the authenticated user. Only fields supplied in the body are touched — missing fields keep their existing values. The server enforces uniqueness on `email` and `username`; conflicts return 409. Always invalidates the in-memory user cache so the next read returns the updated record.

delete.description:
Hard-delete the authenticated user's account. To prove identity at the time of deletion the client signs `delete:{publicKey}:{timestamp}` with the local secp256k1 private key (see `KeyManager.sign` in `@oxyhq/core`). The signature is rejected if it is older than 5 minutes, if the confirmation text does not match the account's username, or if the account has no associated public key.
Successful deletion removes all mailboxes, messages, and S3 attachments owned by the user.

timestamp.description: Unix milliseconds when the signature was produced.

400.description: Missing field, expired signature, or mismatched confirmText.

get.summary: Search for user profiles by username or name
get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/profiles.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

get.summary: Resolve a fediverse handle (e.g. @user@mastodon.social) to an Oxy user profile.
get.description:
Performs WebFinger discovery, fetches the ActivityPub actor, and upserts as a
federated user. Returns cached results if resolved within the last 24 hours.

get.summary: Get profiles similar to a given user, based on co-follower overlap.
get.description: Finds users followed by the same people who follow :userId, ranked by overlap count.

get.summary: Get recommended user profiles based on mutual connections
get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/profiles.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

get.summary: Get aggregated usage statistics across every developer app the user owns
get.description:
for the requested time window (`24h`, `7d`, `30d`, `90d`; defaults to 7d).
Returns totals for requests, tokens, credits, plus a daily breakdown and a
top-10 endpoint breakdown.

get.summary: Developer dashboard overview: app counts, key counts, and last-30-days
get.description: usage totals for the authenticated developer.

get.summary: List the developer's apps (up to 100). Soft-deleted apps are excluded.
get.description:
Each entry includes the app's name, description, redirect URLs, icon, and
active status — but never the API secret or webhook secret.

post.summary: Create a new developer app and return its credentials (apiKey, apiSecret,
post.description:
webhookSecret) — the only time the secrets are returned in plaintext.
Maximum 10 apps per developer.

get.summary: Get a single developer app by ID. Returns 404 if the app does not exist
get.description: or is owned by another developer.

patch.summary: Partially update a developer app. Supplying a new `webhookUrl` also
patch.description:
rotates the `webhookSecret`. `isActive: false` suspends the app so its
keys stop authenticating.

delete.summary: Permanently delete a developer app. Cascade-deletes every API key and
delete.description: usage record tied to the app. Irreversible.

post.summary: Rotate the app's API secret. Returns the new secret in plaintext one
post.description: time — store it immediately, the server cannot reveal it again.

get.summary: Per-app usage statistics over the requested time window (`24h`, `7d`,
get.description:
`30d`, `90d`; defaults to `7d`). Same shape as `/developer/usage` but
filtered to a single app.

get.summary: List API keys for the given app (up to 100). Returns key metadata but
get.description: never the key hash — only the prefix is exposed.

post.summary: Create a new API key for the given app. The plaintext key is returned
post.description:
once and only once — keep it secret. The server stores only its SHA-256
hash and a 16-char prefix.

patch.summary: Update an API key's name, scopes, or active flag. Cannot rotate the key
patch.description: material — delete and recreate for that.

delete.summary: Delete an API key. Cascade-deletes all usage records for the key.
delete.description: Irreversible.

get.summary: Per-key usage statistics over the requested time window.
get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/developer.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.
Query: type, q, limit (max 100), offset, locale
Query: locale
Query: q (required), limit (max 50)
No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/topics.routes.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

patch.summary: Update topic metadata (description, translations, icon, image, aliases).
patch.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/topics.routes.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

post.description: Body: { names: Array<{ name: string; type: TopicType }> }

get.summary: Get all linked authentication methods for the current user
get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/authLinking.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

post.summary: Link a new authentication method to the current user account
post.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/authLinking.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

delete.summary: Unlink an authentication method from the current user account
delete.description: Must keep at least one auth method

post.summary: Proxies chat completion requests to the Alia API.
post.description: Supports both streaming (SSE) and non-streaming responses.

get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/models-stats.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

post.summary: Direct upload via API (bypasses browser CORS for presigned PUT)
post.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/assets.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/assets.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/assets.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

put.summary: Find or create a non-local user (federated, agent, or automated).
put.description:
Called by Oxy ecosystem services when they encounter an external user
that needs an Oxy identity. Requires a valid service token whose
DeveloperApp has been granted the `federation:write` scope.

Hardening (C4):
 - Scope check: rejects service tokens that lack `federation:write`.
 - Actor URI binding: `actorUri.hostname` must match the asserted
   `domain` (so a malicious service can't claim to vouch for a user on
   a host they don't actually own).
 - Username squatting: for `agent` / `automated`, refuse to upsert when
   a `local` (or other-type) user already owns the username.
 - Type immutability: never let `type` change on an existing user — a
   federated user cannot be silently upgraded to an `agent`, etc.

get.description: Public endpoint, no auth needed.
Public endpoint, no auth needed.

post.summary: Create a Stripe checkout session for a one-time credit purchase. Returns
post.description: a session ID and a hosted-page URL to redirect the user to.

post.summary: Create a Stripe checkout session for a subscription plan. Returns the
post.description: checkout URL.

get.summary: Get the user's current active subscription (or `null`).
get.description: No long-form description. Add a JSDoc block (or `@openapi` block) above this route in `src/routes/billing.ts` to fill in summary, description, request/response examples.

post.summary: Cancel the user's current subscription at the end of the billing
post.description:
period. Sets `cancel_at_period_end=true` on the Stripe subscription so
the user keeps access until the period closes.

get.summary: Paginated list of the user's billing transactions (one-time purchases
get.description: and subscription invoices). Default limit 20, max 100.

post.summary: Open a Stripe customer portal session. Returns the portal URL — redirect
post.description:
the user there to manage payment methods, view invoices, or change their
subscription.

post.summary: Stripe webhook receiver. Verifies the `stripe-signature` header against
post.description:
`STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` and dispatches handled events (subscription
created/updated, invoice paid, checkout completed, etc.). No auth.

post.summary: Accepts raw RFC 5322 MIME email as the request body.
post.description:
Content-Type should be message/rfc822 or application/octet-stream.

Headers:
  Authorization: Bearer <EMAIL_INBOUND_WEBHOOK_SECRET>
  X-Envelope-From: sender@example.com (optional, from SMTP MAIL FROM)
  X-Envelope-To: recipient@oxy.so (comma-separated if multiple)

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Sign up, sign in, refresh sessions, 2FA, public-key challenge-response, and FedCM-backed cross-app sign-in. Routes under /auth/*.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Read / update the current user, follow graph, account export and deletion, internal user resolution. Routes under /users/*.

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: Public profile reads (no auth required) — by username, by ID, with similar / recommended profile lookups. Routes under /profiles/*.

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description: Session management: list, validate, refresh, log out one or all sessions on a device. Routes under /session/*.

tags.name: Devices
tags.description: List the user's signed-in devices, inspect security signals, and remotely sign devices out. Routes under /devices/*.

tags.name: Privacy
tags.description: Per-account privacy settings: visibility, blocked users, restricted users. Routes under /privacy/*.

tags.name: Security
tags.description: Account activity log and TOTP-based two-factor authentication setup. Routes under /security/*.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: Privacy-first contact discovery via SHA-256 hashed identifiers. The server never sees raw email or phone. Routes under /contacts/*.

tags.name: Files
tags.description: User-owned file storage — upload, link to entities, sign download URLs, manage variants. Routes under /assets/* and /storage/*.

tags.name: Email
tags.description: Mailboxes, messages, threads, labels, drafts, attachments, and inbound mail webhooks. Routes under /email/*.

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description: In-app notifications and per-channel preferences.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Full-text search across users, profiles, and posts.

tags.name: Developer
tags.description: Developer apps, API keys, webhook subscriptions, usage analytics, service-token registration. Routes under /developer/*.

tags.name: Payments
tags.description: Stripe-backed payments, saved payment methods, refunds.

tags.name: Subscription
tags.description: Subscription plans, billing cycles, customer portal links.

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Stripe webhooks, invoices, charge history.

tags.name: Credits
tags.description: Per-user credit ledger and grants used by AI / paid features.

tags.name: Wallet
tags.description: User wallet — balance, top-up, transfers.

tags.name: Karma
tags.description: Karma scores, contributions, leaderboards.

tags.name: Federation
tags.description: ActivityPub / WebFinger inbox-outbox endpoints and FedCM identity provider routes. Routes under /fedcm/*.

tags.name: AI
tags.description: AI features — Alia assistant, model usage stats.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Read-only analytics for the authenticated account.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Platform health, uptime, public metadata.

tags.name: Misc
tags.description: Miscellaneous endpoints not covered by another group.
